body.title: raise_alarm(fatal=True) — report then halt (throws after the drop)
body.intention: I intend that a should-not-happen event that must NOT allow the caller to continue drops its alarm, then halts the caller by raising — while ordinary alarms stay fail-soft.
body.description:
**Problem:** some should-not-happen events are fatal — the caller must not continue (a corrupt invariant, an unrecoverable boot condition). The primitive is fail-soft by default (never raises), which is right for most alarms but wrong for the fatal class.

**Shape:** add `fatal: bool = False` to `raise_alarm`. When `fatal=True`: drop/report the alarm (synchronous, durable to the flat-file store) and emit the log line as usual, THEN raise `SystemAlarmFatal` to halt the caller (throws once reported, not before; not at all when fatal=False). The out-of-band surfacing (panels/tmux) is unaffected — it reads the store. Default behavior unchanged: fatal=False stays fail-soft and never raises.

**Affected files:** unseen_university/system_alarms.py (add SystemAlarmFatal + fatal param), tests/test_system_alarms.py.
**Design rules:** fail-soft default preserved; fatal is opt-in; the drop happens before the throw (report-then-halt).
**Scope boundary:** IN — the fatal flag + SystemAlarmFatal + report-then-throw ordering. OUT — panel/tmux surfacing (separate tickets); deciding which call sites are fatal.
**Test plan:** fatal=True raises SystemAlarmFatal AND the alarm file is still dropped (reported before the throw); fatal=False (default) never raises and returns an AlarmResult.
**Completion criteria:** raise_alarm(fatal=True) raises SystemAlarmFatal after the artifact is written + log emitted; raise_alarm(fatal=False) returns normally and writes the artifact; SystemAlarmFatal is importable from the module.

body.result: raise_alarm(fatal=True) reports then halts (drops+logs, then raises SystemAlarmFatal); fatal=False stays fail-soft. Proof: tests/test_system_alarms.py::test_fatal_drops_then_raises @ 87de2157 (stub-first red→green).
